WEEK 2 · 20.2 · HOOK
TALK

Equivalent fractions

Pizza panic!

Ava ate 12. Noah ate 24.

Who ate more — or did they eat the same amount?
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
In 35, what does the 5 tell us?
2
Which is larger: 13 or 16?
3
What does equal mean?
Tomorrow: mixed numerals — the same amount named as wholes + parts.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🍕

We are learning to…

recognise and create fractions that name the same amount

Words we will use

equivalentnumeratordenominatorvalue

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Same amount. Different name.

12
=
24
=
48
Equivalent fractions have the same value. Multiply or divide the numerator and denominator by the same non-zero number.
REVEALED ANSWER
12 = 24 = 48.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: “4/8 is bigger than 1/2 because 4 and 8 are bigger numbers.”
Draw both amountsSame shaded portion?Rule: ×/÷ top and bottom by the same number
REVEALED ANSWER
False — 4/8 = 1/2. Bigger digits do not mean a bigger fraction when the whole is partitioned differently.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Prove that 36 = 12.
Draw a barShade 3 of 6Regroup into halves
REVEALED ANSWER
36 shades half the whole, so it is equivalent to 12.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

23 and 46
25 and 48
34 and 68
Thumbs up if equivalent. Thumbs down if not. Justify one.
REVEALED ANSWER
Equivalent: 23 and 46; 34 and 68. Not equivalent: 25 and 48.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportMatch shaded bars for 1/2 and 2/4 only.
On-trackWorkbook pages as set (models before the rule).
StretchFind five fractions equivalent to 1 2 .

Workbook pages 132–133

Complete Questions 1–4. Use the models before using the rule.

□ Count equal parts
□ Count shaded parts
□ Check the amount stayed the same
SOAKER Find five fractions equivalent to 12.
REVEALED ANSWER
Question 2: 13 = 26 = 39 = 412.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Are 35 and 610 equivalent?
2
Explain your reasoning in one sentence.
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
Yes. Both numerator and denominator were multiplied by 2.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Tonight’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Due next lesson · answers unlocked in W2L2 · slide 2
112 = ?/4
212 = ?/6
312 = ?/8
412 = ?/10
513 = ?/6
613 = ?/9
714 = ?/8
814 = ?/12
923 = ?/6
1023 = ?/12
1134 = ?/8
1235 = ?/10
13Are 24 and 12 equivalent?
14Are 25 and 48 equivalent?
15Are 36 and 12 equivalent?
16Are 23 and 46 equivalent?
1712 = ?/4
1812 = ?/8
1923 = ?/6
20Are 36 and 12 equivalent?
WEEK 2 · 21.1 · HOOK
TALK

Mixed numerals and improper fractions

Seven quarter-pies arrive.

The label says 74. A customer says, “That cannot be right — the top is bigger!”

How many whole pies and quarters are there?
HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W2L1
TEAMS

Mark last night’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
112 = ?/42/4
212 = ?/63/6
312 = ?/84/8
412 = ?/105/10
513 = ?/62/6
613 = ?/93/9
714 = ?/82/8
814 = ?/123/12
923 = ?/64/6
1023 = ?/128/12
1134 = ?/86/8
1235 = ?/106/10
13Are 24 and 12 equivalent?Yes
14Are 25 and 48 equivalent?No
15Are 36 and 12 equivalent?Yes
16Are 23 and 46 equivalent?Yes
1712 = ?/42/4
1812 = ?/84/8
1923 = ?/64/6
20Are 36 and 12 equivalent?Yes
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
How many 14 pieces make one whole?
2
What does 44 equal?
3
What happens when the numerator is greater than the denominator?
Tomorrow: place value to thousandths — every digit has an address.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🥧

We are learning to…

move between mixed numerals and improper fractions

Words we will use

mixed numeralimproper fractionwholeremainder

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Two ways to name the same amount

74improper fraction
7 ÷ 4 = 1 remainder 3
⇄
134mixed numeral
Divide the numerator by the denominator. The quotient is the whole number; the remainder becomes the new numerator.
REVEALED ANSWER
74 = 134.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: “7/4 is wrong because the top is bigger than the bottom.”
Improper = more than one wholeConvert: ÷ then remainderCheck: mixed → improper
REVEALED ANSWER
7/4 is a valid improper fraction: 1 whole and 3/4 left over → 1 3/4.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Convert 114 to a mixed numeral, then convert it back.
Make wholesUse division with remainderCheck by multiplying
REVEALED ANSWER
11 ÷ 4 = 2 r3, so 114 = 234. Check: 2 × 4 + 3 = 11.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

95 = ?
312 = ?
143 = ?
234 = ?
Say the conversion method, not only the answer.
REVEALED ANSWER
95=145; 312=72; 143=423; 234=114.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportUse pizza wedges: build 5/4 then rename as mixed.
On-trackWorkbook conversions both ways.
StretchCreate two different improper fractions between 3 and 4.

Draw and convert

  1. Draw 136 using bars or circles.
  2. Write it as a mixed numeral.
  3. Convert 425 to an improper fraction.
  4. Explain one method.
✏️
Quiet maths
Show every step.
SOAKER Create two different improper fractions between 3 and 4.
REVEALED ANSWER
136=216; 425=225.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Convert 175 to a mixed numeral.
2
Convert 223 to an improper fraction.
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
175=325; 223=83.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Tonight’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Due next lesson · answers unlocked in W2L3 · slide 2

8Convert 175 to mixed.

11Convert 3 12 to improper.

13Convert 2 34 to improper.
14Convert 4 15 to improper.

16Convert 53 to mixed.

WEEK 2 · 7.2 · HOOK
TALK

Place value to thousandths

Photo finish!

Two runners finish in 12.347 s and 12.374 s.

Those digits look almost identical. Which place decides the race?
HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W2L2
TEAMS

Mark last night’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
1Convert 74 to mixed.1 3/4
2Convert 53 to mixed.1 2/3
3Convert 114 to mixed.2 3/4
4Convert 92 to mixed.4 1/2
5Convert 85 to mixed.1 3/5
6Convert 103 to mixed.3 1/3
7Convert 135 to mixed.2 3/5
8Convert 175 to mixed.3 2/5
9Convert 1 14 to improper.5/4
10Convert 2 13 to improper.7/3
11Convert 3 12 to improper.7/2
12Convert 1 25 to improper.7/5
13Convert 2 34 to improper.11/4
14Convert 4 15 to improper.21/5
15Convert 74 to mixed.1 3/4
16Convert 53 to mixed.1 2/3
17Convert 1 14 to improper.5/4
18Convert 2 13 to improper.7/3
19Which is larger: 74 or 1 12?7/4
20Is 94 more than 2?Yes (2 1/4)
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
What is one tenth written as a decimal?
2
Which place is immediately right of the tenths?
3
Why can a zero be important in a decimal?
Tomorrow: comparing decimals — left to right, place by place.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

⏱️

We are learning to…

read, write and represent decimals to three decimal places

Words we will use

tenthshundredthsthousandthsplaceholder

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Every digit has an address

Ones
Tenths
Hundredths
Thousandths
4
2
0
6
4.206 = 4 ones + 2 tenths + 0 hundredths + 6 thousandths
The decimal point does not move. Read each digit by its place.
REVEALED ANSWER
4.206 is four and two hundred and six thousandths.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: “The decimal point moves when we rename thousandths.”
Point stays putName each placeRead the number aloud
REVEALED ANSWER
The point does not move. Digits shift meaning by place: tenths, hundredths, thousandths.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Represent 7.035 in a place-value chart and write it in words.
Line up the decimal pointKeep the zeroName the final place
REVEALED ANSWER
7.035 is seven and thirty-five thousandths: 7 ones, 0 tenths, 3 hundredths and 5 thousandths.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

Write 0.408 in words
Write “six and nine thousandths” as a decimal
What is the value of 7 in 3.172?
Which is 52 thousandths: 0.052 or 0.520?
Explain the job of every zero.
REVEALED ANSWER
0.408 = four hundred and eight thousandths; 6.009; 0.07 or seven hundredths; 0.052.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportPlace-value grid for one number only (e.g. 4.206).
On-trackWorkbook place-value questions as set.
StretchMake the smallest decimal possible using 2, 5 and 8 once each after the decimal point.

Place-value code breaker

  1. Draw a four-column chart.
  2. Represent 2.507, 0.064 and 13.090.
  3. Write each in words.
  4. Circle the thousandths digit.
🔐
Do not drop zeros
They hold places open.
SOAKER Make the smallest decimal possible using 2, 5 and 8 once each after the decimal point.
REVEALED ANSWER
2.507: two and five hundred and seven thousandths; 0.064: sixty-four thousandths; 13.090: thirteen and ninety thousandths.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Write “nine and forty-two thousandths” as a decimal.
2
What is the value of the 6 in 8.361?
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
9.042; the 6 is six hundredths, or 0.06.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

7Write “six and eight hundredths”.
8Write “two and fifteen thousandths”.

10What is the value of 3 in 5.273?

13Is 0.50 equal to 0.05?
14Write 0.7 as thousandths.
15Value of the 6 in 4.206?
16Write “nine and forty-two thousandths”.
17What is the value of 7 in 5.273?
18Is 0.50 equal to 0.5?
19Write “six and eight hundredths”.
20Order from smallest: 0.4, 0.04, 0.004
WEEK 2 · 21.2 · HOOK
TALK

Comparing decimals

Fastest wins.

Mia: 9.8 s
Leo: 9.75 s

A student says 9.8 is smaller because 8 is smaller than 75. Convince them.
HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W2L3
TEAMS

Mark last night’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
1Value of the 6 in 4.206?6 thousandths
2Value of the 2 in 4.206?2 tenths
3Value of the 0 in 4.206?0 hundredths
4Write “four and two hundred and six thousandths”.4.206
5Write “nine and forty-two thousandths”.9.042
6Write “three and five tenths”.3.5
7Write “six and eight hundredths”.6.08
8Write “two and fifteen thousandths”.2.015
9What is the value of 7 in 5.273?7 hundredths
10What is the value of 3 in 5.273?3 thousandths
11Order from smallest: 0.4, 0.04, 0.0040.004, 0.04, 0.4
12Is 0.50 equal to 0.5?Yes
13Is 0.50 equal to 0.05?No
14Write 0.7 as thousandths.0.700
15Value of the 6 in 4.206?6 thousandths
16Write “nine and forty-two thousandths”.9.042
17What is the value of 7 in 5.273?7 hundredths
18Is 0.50 equal to 0.5?Yes
19Write “six and eight hundredths”.6.08
20Order from smallest: 0.4, 0.04, 0.0040.004, 0.04, 0.4
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
What does 0.7 equal in hundredths?
2
Which is larger: 0.4 or 0.04?
3
Where must decimal points be placed when comparing?
Tomorrow: decimals, fractions and percentages — three names for one amount.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🏁

We are learning to…

compare and order decimals using place value and number lines

Words we will use

compareorderplaceholder zerogreater than

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Compare from left to right

3.07
3.70
1. Line up decimal points.
2. Add placeholder zeros if helpful.
3. Compare ones, then tenths, hundredths, thousandths.
3.07 < 3.70 because 0 tenths is less than 7 tenths.
REVEALED ANSWER
3.07 is less than 3.7.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: “0.408 is larger than 0.48 because it has more digits.”
Line up pointsCompare left → rightAdd trailing zeros
REVEALED ANSWER
0.408 < 0.480. More digits does not mean larger — compare place by place.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Compare 4.205 and 4.25. Show the place-value comparison.
Write 4.250Compare onesThen tenths, hundredths, thousandths
REVEALED ANSWER
4.205 < 4.250 because the tenths are equal, but 0 hundredths is less than 5 hundredths.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

0.56 ☐ 0.506
2.09 ☐ 2.9
1.375 ☐ 1.357
0.8 ☐ 0.800
Insert <, > or =.
REVEALED ANSWER
0.56 > 0.506; 2.09 < 2.9; 1.375 > 1.357; 0.8 = 0.800.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportRewrite both with 3 d.p., then compare.
On-trackWorkbook compare/order as set.
StretchCreate three decimals between 0.39 and 0.40.

Order the race times

Order from fastest to slowest:

12.48   12.405   12.5   12.045   12.450

Remember: the smallest time is fastest.

🏊
Line them up
Add zeros where needed.
SOAKER Create three decimals between 0.39 and 0.40.
REVEALED ANSWER
12.045, 12.405, 12.450, 12.48, 12.5.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Which is greater: 5.099 or 5.1?
2
Explain by adding placeholder zeros.
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
5.1 is greater because 5.100 > 5.099.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE

19Larger: 0.3 or 0.8?

WEEK 3 · Decimals · HOOK
TALK

Decimals, fractions and percentages

Battery mystery

A tablet shows 50%. Another shows 0.5. A third shows 12.

Which has the most charge?
HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W2L4
TEAMS

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.

2Larger: 0.6 or 0.59?0.6
3Larger: 0.408 or 0.48?0.48
4Larger: 5.099 or 5.1?5.1
5Larger: 3.07 or 3.7?3.7
6Larger: 0.09 or 0.1?0.1
7Order: 0.4, 0.04, 0.440.04, 0.4, 0.44
8Order: 1.2, 1.02, 1.201.02, 1.2 = 1.20
9Rewrite 0.4 with 2 d.p.0.40
10Rewrite 0.7 with 3 d.p.0.700

12Larger: 5.099 or 5.1?5.1
13Larger: 3.07 or 3.7?3.7

WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
What fraction is 25 out of 100?
2
Write 12 as a decimal.
3
What does percent mean?
Tomorrow: SA5 revision — shade, order, label, explain under pressure.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🔋

We are learning to…

connect common fractions, decimals and percentages

Words we will use

percentout of 100decimalequivalent

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Percent means “out of 100”

25%
=
0.25
=
25100 = 14
To change a percentage to a decimal, divide by 100 — move two place values to the left.
REVEALED ANSWER
25% = 0.25 = 25100 = 14.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: “25% = 25.0 as a decimal.”
% means /100Move 2 places leftCheck with a fraction
REVEALED ANSWER
25% = 25/100 = 0.25 (not 25).
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Represent 35% as a decimal and a fraction. Shade it on a hundred grid.
35 out of 100Write 0.35Simplify if possible
REVEALED ANSWER
35% = 0.35 = 35100 = 720.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

34 = ?%
0.6 = ?%
10% = decimal?
15 = decimal and %?
Use a hundred as your bridge.
REVEALED ANSWER
34=75%; 0.6=60%; 10%=0.10; 15=0.2=20%.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportPercent → fraction /100 → decimal for 10%, 50%, 25%.
On-trackWorkbook % / decimal / fraction links.
StretchFind a percentage between one third and one half.

Match three names

Create matching fraction–decimal–percentage triples for:

  1. one half
  2. one quarter
  3. three tenths
  4. nine tenths
🔗
Same value
Different names.
SOAKER Find a percentage between one third and one half.
REVEALED ANSWER
12=0.5=50%; 14=0.25=25%; 310=0.3=30%; 910=0.9=90%.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Write 65% as a decimal and fraction out of 100.
2
Which is larger: 0.7 or 65%?
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
65%=0.65=65100; 0.7=70%, so 0.7 is larger.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Tonight’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

1150% as a fraction

1565% as /100

180.25 as a %

TALK

SA5 revision: fractions and decimals

The Assessment Vault

Six locks. Each lock needs a different fraction or decimal skill.

You do not need speed. You need organised working and sharp place value.
HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W3L1
TEAMS

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
125% as a decimal0.25
250% as a decimal0.5
310% as a decimal0.1
475% as a decimal0.75
520% as a decimal0.2
65% as a decimal0.05
70.25 as a %25%
80.5 as a %50%
90.1 as a %10%
1025% as a fraction /10025/100 = 1/4
1150% as a fraction50/100 = 1/2
12Larger: 0.7 or 65%?0.7
13Larger: 40% or 0.45?0.45
1465% as a decimal0.65
1565% as /10065/100
1625% as a decimal0.25
1750% as a decimal0.5
180.25 as a %25%
19Larger: 0.7 or 65%?0.7
2010% as a decimal0.1
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
Convert 1410 to a mixed numeral.
2
Which is greater: 0.60 or 0.06?
3
Calculate 38 + 28.
Next: SA5 assessment sessions — bring calm strategies and clear working.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🧩

We are learning to…

prepare for SA5: shade, place value, order on a number line, add/subtract fractions, and explain.

shadehundredthsorderrelatedexplain

I’ll know I’ve got it when I can…

  • shade fractions and hundredths grids accurately
  • place decimals and fractions on a vertical tenths line
  • add/subtract same-denominator fractions fluently
  • solve a mixed-number compare/apply problem and justify
EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

What SA5 will ask you to do

Represent fractions and decimals
Use place value
Order numbers on a number line
Compare mixed numerals
Add and subtract related fractions
Explain your reasoning
Read the command word: shade, show, order, label, calculate, explain.
REVEALED ANSWER
Every question needs the matching mathematical action.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: “0.06 is larger than 0.60 because it has a 6 in the hundredths and looks busier.”
Say the places aloudShade both on a hundredths gridLine up the points
REVEALED ANSWER
0.60 > 0.06. Sixty hundredths is ten times six hundredths.
SA5 PREP · SHADE FRACTIONS
NOTEBOOK

Shade and compare

Shade 28

Shade exactly two of eight equal parts.

Shade 58

Shade five of eight. Which is greater — and why?

REVEALED ANSWER
58 is greater than 28 — more equal parts of the same whole are shaded.
SA5 PREP · HUNDREDTHS GRIDS
NOTEBOOK

Decimals on a hundredths grid

Shade 0.30

30 hundredths — shade 30 small squares.

Shade 0.03

3 hundredths — shade 3. Which is greater?

REVEALED ANSWER
0.30 is greater than 0.03. Thirty hundredths > three hundredths.
SA5 PREP · PLACE VALUE
NOTEBOOK

Build each number

a. 47 hundredths

OnesTenthsHundredths
   

b. Two and 8 hundredths

OnesTenthsHundredths
   

c. Six and 15 hundredths

OnesTenthsHundredths
   
REVEALED ANSWER
a) 0.47   b) 2.08   c) 6.15
SA5 PREP · ORDER DECIMALS
NOTEBOOK

Smallest to greatest

Order from smallest to greatest:

1.31
0.03
1.13
0.30
2.01
1.03
REVEALED ANSWER
0.03 , 0.30 , 1.03 , 1.13 , 1.31 , 2.01
SA5 PREP · NUMBER LINE
NOTEBOOK

Fractions on the vertical line

Label these fractions on the left (convert first if needed): 310, 1210, 45, 1 15, 1 710, 2 210

Fractions (left)
012
Decimals (right)
REVEALED ANSWER
310=0.3; 1210=1.2; 45=0.8; 1 15=1.2; 1 710=1.7; 2 210=2.2
SA5 PREP · REASONING
TALK

Place these on the line

Place 3 14, 3 58 and 4 12 on the number line from 3 to 5.
Order them and explain how you know.
345

Place each mixed number. Convert first if it helps.

REVEALED ANSWER
3 14=3.25; 3 58=3.625; 4 12=4.5. Order: 3 14, then 3 58, then 4 12.
SA5 PREP · FLUENCY
NOTEBOOK

Same-denominator + and −

27 + 37 =
110 + 610 =
78 − 38 =
910 − 410 =
1012 − 412 =
16 + 46 =
25 + 25 =
68 − 18 =
REVEALED ANSWER
57; 710; 48=12; 510=12; 612=12; 56; 45; 58
SA5 PREP · RELATED FRACTIONS
NOTEBOOK

Ribbon lengths

Sam needs 1 310 metres of the same-coloured ribbon.
Available: yellow 1 15 · purple 1 12 · orange 1 25.
Which colour(s) are long enough? Show working.
REVEALED ANSWER
Need 1 310. Yellow 1 15=1 210 too short; purple 1 12=1 510 enough; orange 1 25=1 410 enough. Purple or orange.
SA5 PREP · APPLY
NOTEBOOK

Faster than 28½ seconds

Runners faster than 28 12 seconds qualify for the final. Who qualifies? Show working.
RunnerTime (s)
Jordan28.45
Kai28.55
Riley28.60
Morgan28.05
Avery27.95
Quinn28.52
REVEALED ANSWER
28 12=28.5. Faster means < 28.5 → Jordan 28.45, Morgan 28.05, Avery 27.95.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Order these from smallest to greatest: 0.6, 710, 0.65, 35.
Convert to a common formKeep equal values togetherUse < or a number line
REVEALED ANSWER
35=0.6, so 0.6 and 35 are equal; then 0.65; then 710=0.7.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

110+610
78−38
Order 1 15, 1210, 0.8
Thumbs / whiteboards — justify one answer.
REVEALED ANSWER
7/10; 4/8=1/2; order 0.8, 1 1/5 (=1.2), 12/10 (=1.2) — discuss equality of last two.
ASSESSMENT STRATEGY
WATCH

Assessment calm-down check

① Read the whole question
② Circle the command word
③ Show working
④ Check place value
⑤ Re-read your answer
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportHighlight command words on two sample questions.
On-trackRevision set as planned.
StretchWrite your own SA5-style question and its answer.
Station 1
Shade 0.37 on a hundred grid.
Station 2
Place 1.25, 0.8 and 1.05 on a number line.
Station 3
Explain whether 2 1/5 m is enough for 2.4 m.
Station 4
Calculate 11/12 − 5/12.
SOAKER Write your own SA5-style question and its answer.
REVEALED ANSWER
Station 3: 215=2.2, so it is not enough for 2.4 m. Station 4: 612=12.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Which is greater: 0.30 or 0.03? Why?
2
27 + 37 = ?
Tomorrow: SA5 Session 1 — bring calm strategies and clear working.
Write in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
1) 0.30 — thirty hundredths > three hundredths. 2) 5/7

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

SA5 · Session 1

SA5

Representing and ordering fractions and decimals

This is individual, supervised work. Read each command word and show working where requested.

10110 + 610 =7/10
1178 − 38 =4/8 = 1/2
12910 − 410 =5/10 = 1/2

1827 + 37 =5/7

ASSESSMENT CRITERIA
WATCH

What success looks like

Understanding
Represent fractions with the same or related denominators.
Fluency
Use place value to write and order decimals, including decimals greater than one.
Accuracy
Use diagrams, place-value charts and number lines carefully.
SUPERVISED WORK
ASSESS

Assessment page 1

Work directly in your assessment document

Use this screen only to enlarge the page or clarify where you are up to.

□ Shade exactly the requested amount.
□ Line up decimal place values.
□ Order from smallest to greatest.
□ Check that all labels are clear.
FILES
WATCH

Assessment files

Everything is inside this HTML file.

Use these buttons to save a fresh copy of the assessment when needed.

Download SA5 DOCXDownload SA5 PDF
FINAL CHECK
ASSESS

Before you submit

Shade exactly the requested amount.
Line up decimal place values.
Order from smallest to greatest.
Check that all labels are clear.
My name is on the document.
I have answered every page assigned today.
Do not reveal or discuss answers during supervised assessment.

SA5 · Session 2

SA5

Number lines, reasoning and calculations

Continue from where you stopped. Show working for comparison problems and calculations.

ASSESSMENT CRITERIA
WATCH

What success looks like

Order fractions
Use fractions with the same or related denominators.
Calculate
Add and subtract fractions with the same or related denominators.
Explain
Use mathematical evidence, not a guess.

□ Use equivalent forms when comparing.

□ Simplify final fraction answers where appropriate.

WEEK 4 · 7.1 · HOOK
TALK

Multiplication using split

Snack-box emergency

There are 6 classes. Each needs 23 snack boxes.

How can 20 + 3 make the multiplication easier?
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
What is 6 × 3?
2
What is 6 × 20?
3
How can 47 be partitioned?
Tomorrow: 4-digit × 1-digit — place value carries the load.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🧱

We are learning to…

use partitioning and the distributive property to multiply efficiently

Words we will use

partitionpartial productrecombinedistributive

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Split, multiply, recombine

23 × 6
= (20 × 6) + (3 × 6)
= 120 + 18
= 138
The distributive property lets us split one factor into friendly parts.
REVEALED ANSWER
23 × 6 = 138.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: “23 × 6 = 20 × 6 + 3” (forgot to multiply the ones).
Split both partsMultiply eachAdd partial products
REVEALED ANSWER
23 × 6 = (20 × 6) + (3 × 6) = 120 + 18 = 138. Every part must be multiplied.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Solve 47 × 6 using a split strategy.
Split 47 into 40 + 7Multiply each partAdd partial products
REVEALED ANSWER
47 × 6 = 240 + 42 = 282.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

32 × 7
64 × 5
109 × 4
58 × 6
Write the split beside every calculation.
REVEALED ANSWER
224; 320; 436; 348.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportOne split only: tens + ones for 23 × 6.
On-trackWorkbook split strategy as set.
StretchFind two different splits for 48 × 7 and prove they give the same answer.

Choose your split

  1. 38 × 9
  2. 126 × 5
  3. 207 × 8
  4. Explain which split was easiest.
✂️
Break it apart
Then put it back together.
SOAKER Find two different splits for 48 × 7 and prove they give the same answer.
REVEALED ANSWER
342; 630; 1656.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Use split multiplication to calculate 76 × 4.
2
Name the property that makes the split valid.
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
76 × 4 = 280 + 24 = 304; the distributive property.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Tonight’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Due next lesson · answers unlocked in W4L2 · slide 2

159 × 14 (split)

1723 = 20 + ?
1815 = 10 + ?
195 × 28 (split)
207 × 21 (split)

A school orders 2,437 stickers for each of 4 houses.

Estimate first. Should the answer be closer to 8,000 or 80,000?

WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
4 × 7
2
4 × 30
3
Estimate 2,437 to the nearest thousand.
Tomorrow: × tens and hundreds — scale after the non-zero digits.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🚀

We are learning to…

multiply large numbers by one digit using place value and regrouping

Words we will use

regroupplace valueestimatepartial product

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Multiply by place value

2437
× 4
9748
Ones: 4 × 7 = 28
Tens: 4 × 3 tens + 2 tens
Hundreds, then thousands
Estimate to check
REVEALED ANSWER
2,437 × 4 = 9,748.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: Skipping a place-value column when multiplying 2,437 × 4.
Ones → thousandsWrite carries clearlyEstimate first
REVEALED ANSWER
Keep every column. Estimate ~2,400 × 4 = 9,600 to sense-check 9,748.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Calculate 3,204 × 7. Explain how the zero affects the calculation.
Start with onesRegroup carefullyKeep the zero place
REVEALED ANSWER
3,204 × 7 = 22,428. The zero means there are no tens before regrouping.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

4,126 × 3
2,508 × 6
7,030 × 5
1,999 × 4
Estimate before calculating exactly.
REVEALED ANSWER
12,378; 15,048; 35,150; 7,996.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportGrid method for one 4-digit × 1-digit.
On-trackWorkbook algorithm as set.
StretchCreate a 4-digit × 1-digit calculation with an answer between 20,000 and 21,000.

Accuracy ladder

  1. Estimate 5,607 × 8.
  2. Calculate exactly.
  3. Check using a split method.
  4. Explain whether the exact answer is reasonable.
🪜
Estimate → solve → check
SOAKER Create a 4-digit × 1-digit calculation with an answer between 20,000 and 21,000.
REVEALED ANSWER
Estimate about 48,000; exact answer 44,856; split check: 40,000 + 4,800 + 56 = 44,856.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Calculate 4,305 × 6.
2
Give a sensible estimate.
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
25,830; estimate about 24,000 or 26,000 depending on rounding.

Retrieval sprint

1
36 × 4
2
7 × 100
3
What is the value of 4 in 40?
Tomorrow: area model — four rectangles, one product.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🏭

We are learning to…

multiply efficiently by multiples of 10 and 100 using place value

Words we will use

multiple of tenmultiple of hundredscaleplace-value shift

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Multiply the non-zero digits, then scale

36 × 40
= 36 × 4 × 10
= 144 × 10
= 1,440
Do not say “just add a zero” without understanding the place-value shift.
REVEALED ANSWER
36 × 40 = 1,440.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: “Just add a zero” with no place-value meaning.
×10 moves one place×100 moves twoNon-zero digits first
REVEALED ANSWER
36 × 40 = 36 × 4 × 10. The zero is a place-value scale, not decoration.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Calculate 27 × 300 using place value and a written chain.
27 × 3Then × 100Check digit places
REVEALED ANSWER
27 × 300 = 27 × 3 × 100 = 81 × 100 = 8,100.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

48 × 20
63 × 500
240 × 30
1,204 × 60
Show the scale factor.
REVEALED ANSWER
960; 31,500; 7,200; 72,240.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportShow 36 × 4 then ×10 on a place chart.
On-trackWorkbook ×10/×100 chains.
StretchWithout calculating exactly, decide whether 398 × 60 is above or below 24,000.

Sort and solve

Group these as ×10, ×100 or both:

34×70   56×400   125×80   903×200

Then calculate.

🔟
Place value matters
SOAKER Without calculating exactly, decide whether 398 × 60 is above or below 24,000.
REVEALED ANSWER
2,380; 22,400; 10,000; 180,600.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Calculate 86 × 700.
2
Explain where the two place-value shifts came from.
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
60,200. First 86 × 7 = 602, then × 100 because 700 is 7 hundreds.

1
Split 23 into tens and ones.
2

3
What does area mean?
Tomorrow: 3-digit × 2-digit — ones row, tens row, combine.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🟦

We are learning to…

use an area model to organise partial products

Words we will use

area modelfactorpartial productsum

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Four rectangles, one product

20
3
10
200
30
4
80
12
200 + 30 + 80 + 12 = 322
REVEALED ANSWER
23 × 14 = 322.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: Adding only two of the four area-model rectangles.
Split both factorsFour productsAdd all four
REVEALED ANSWER
23 × 14 needs four partial products (20×10, 20×4, 3×10, 3×4) then a total.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Use an area model to calculate 36 × 27.
Split 36 into 30 + 6Split 27 into 20 + 7Add four partial products
REVEALED ANSWER
600 + 210 + 120 + 42 = 972.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

42 × 15
58 × 23
31 × 26
74 × 12
List all four partial products.
REVEALED ANSWER
630; 1,334; 806; 888.

NOTEBOOK

Your turn

On-trackWorkbook area models as set.

Draw it yourself

  1. Draw an area model for 64 × 35.
  2. Label every side.
  3. Fill four products.
  4. Add and estimate-check.
📐
Neat labels
Every box has meaning.
SOAKER Explain how the area model connects to the vertical algorithm.
REVEALED ANSWER
64 × 35 = 1,800 + 120 + 400 + 80 = 2,400.

128 × 4
2

Estimate 142 × 18 using 140 × 20.
Tomorrow: multiplication word problems — R-E-A-D before you calculate.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🎟️

We are learning to…

multiply a three-digit number by a two-digit number accurately

Words we will use

two-digit factorpartial productregroupestimate

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

One-digit row, tens row, combine

128
× 24
512
2560
3072
128 × 4 = 512
128 × 20 = 2,560
Add partial products
Estimate-check
REVEALED ANSWER
142 × 18 = 2,556.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: Treating the tens digit as ones (e.g. 24 × as if 4 only).
Ones rowTens row (×10)Add rows
REVEALED ANSWER
For ×24: multiply by 4, then by 20 (or 2 tens), then add. The tens row is ten times larger.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Calculate 243 × 16. Show both partial products.
243 × 6243 × 10Add and estimate-check
REVEALED ANSWER
1,458 + 2,430 = 3,888.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

312 × 14
205 × 32
146 × 25
408 × 17
Keep the tens-row place value visible.
REVEALED ANSWER
4,368; 6,560; 3,650; 6,936.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportColour-code ones row vs tens row.
On-trackWorkbook 3-digit × 2-digit as set.
StretchCreate a 3-digit × 2-digit calculation with a product close to 10,000.

Double-method check

  1. Solve 326 × 28 vertically.
  2. Check using an area model.
  3. Write an estimate.
  4. Circle any regrouping.
🔁
Two methods
One answer.
SOAKER Create a 3-digit × 2-digit calculation with a product close to 10,000.
REVEALED ANSWER
326 × 28 = 9,128. Partial products: 6,520 and 2,608.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Calculate 214 × 35.
2
What is the value of the 3 in 35?
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
7,490; the 3 represents 30.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Tonight’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

2215 × 13
3106 × 25

7111 × 22
8150 × 14
9203 × 12
10Ones digit of 24 means ×?
11Tens digit of 24 means ×?
12142 × 18
13214 × 35
14215 × 13
15106 × 25
16132 × 16
17240 × 15

19150 × 14
20203 × 12
WEEK 5 · Problems · HOOK
TALK

Multiplication problems

Too much information!

A problem includes prices, times, colours and quantities.

Which details matter — and which operation will solve the question?
HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W5L1
TEAMS

Mark last night’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
1142 × 182556
2215 × 132795
3106 × 252650
4214 × 357490
5132 × 162112
6240 × 153600
7111 × 222442
8150 × 142100
9203 × 122436
10Ones digit of 24 means ×?×4
11Tens digit of 24 means ×?×20
12142 × 182556
13214 × 357490
14215 × 132795
15106 × 252650
16132 × 162112
17240 × 153600
18111 × 222442
19150 × 142100
20203 × 122436
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
What words might suggest equal groups?
2
Why should you estimate before solving?
3
What does the question actually ask for?
Tomorrow: choosing units — attribute first, then sensible size.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🕵️

We are learning to…

choose multiplication, organise information and justify a solution

Words we will use

rateequal groupsrelevant informationreasonable

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

R-E-A-D the problem

R Read twice
E Extract useful facts
A Ask: what operation?
D Do, then check
Do not choose multiplication because you saw a big number. Choose it because the situation has equal groups or scaling.
REVEALED ANSWER
Use the situation, not a keyword alone.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: Multiplying because the problem has two big numbers.
R-E-A-DEqual groups?Cross out extras
REVEALED ANSWER
Choose the operation from the situation (equal groups / arrays), not from keywords or number size.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

A tap drips 128 times each hour. How many drips occur in 24 hours?
Underline rate and timeWrite 115 × 24Estimate, solve, label
REVEALED ANSWER
115 × 24 = 2,760 drips.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

42 drops each minute for 5 minutes
180 drips each hour for 4 hours
95 drips each hour; another tap is twice as fast
40 mL every 3 minutes; scale to 12 minutes
Write the equation before calculating.
REVEALED ANSWER
210 drops; 720 drips; faster rate 190 drips/hour; 160 mL.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportSort 3 cards: multiply / divide / extra info.
On-trackWord-problem set as planned.
StretchWrite a multiplication problem that has one irrelevant fact.

SA6-style problem

A leaking tap loses 135 mL every 3 minutes.

  1. How much in 12 minutes?
  2. How much in one hour?
  3. Explain the scaling steps.
💧
Label units
Every answer needs context.
SOAKER Write a multiplication problem that has one irrelevant fact.
REVEALED ANSWER
12 minutes is 4 groups: 540 mL. One hour is 20 groups of 3 minutes: 2,700 mL or 2.7 L.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
A box holds 48 bottles. How many bottles in 26 boxes?
2
Write one sentence explaining why multiplication fits.
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
1,248 bottles. There are 26 equal groups of 48.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Tonight’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

215 rows × 24 seats?
38 packs × 125 stickers?
412 × 35 = ?
5Does “altogether” always mean add?
6Equal groups → which operation?
7Why cross out irrelevant facts?

915 × 24
108 × 125

1930 × 18
2025 × 16
WEEK 5 · 25.3 · HOOK
TALK

Choosing units of measure

Ridiculous measurements

A swimming pool holds 2,000 millilitres. A pencil is 18 kilometres long.

What makes a unit sensible?
HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W5L2
TEAMS

Mark last night’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
148 bottles × 26 boxes?1248 bottles
215 rows × 24 seats?360 seats
38 packs × 125 stickers?1000 stickers
412 × 35 = ?420
5Does “altogether” always mean add?No — read the story
6Equal groups → which operation?Multiplication
7Why cross out irrelevant facts?They don’t change the maths
848 × 261248
915 × 24360
108 × 1251000
1112 × 35420
1230 × 18540
1325 × 16400
1448 × 261248
1515 × 24360
1612 × 35420
17Equal groups → which operation?Multiplication
188 × 1251000
1930 × 18540
2025 × 16400
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
Which unit suits a pencil: mm, cm or km?
2
Which unit suits a person’s mass: g or kg?
3
Which unit suits a bucket: mL or L?
Tomorrow: litres and millilitres — read the scale, don’t guess the picture.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

📏

We are learning to…

select sensible metric units for length, mass and capacity

Words we will use

attributeunitcapacityreasonable

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Choose by attribute and size

Lengthmm · cm · m · km
Massg · kg
CapacitymL · L
First identify what is being measured. Then choose a unit that gives a practical-sized number.
REVEALED ANSWER
A bottle is usually measured in millilitres or litres, not grams.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: Measuring a swimming pool in millilitres.
What attribute?Sensible size?Practical number
REVEALED ANSWER
Match unit to attribute (capacity vs length vs mass) and choose a unit that gives a sensible-sized number.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Choose and justify a unit for: a classroom, a raindrop, a dog, and a road trip.
Name the attributeThink about sizeExplain why
REVEALED ANSWER
Classroom length: metres; raindrop capacity: millilitres; dog mass: kilograms; road distance: kilometres.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

Length of an ant
Mass of an apple
Capacity of a bath
Distance Brisbane to Gold Coast
Choose the best unit and explain one.
REVEALED ANSWER
Millimetres; grams; litres; kilometres.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportSort objects into L / mL / m / km cards.
On-trackUnit-choice workbook tasks.
StretchFind an object that could sensibly be measured in either mL or L.

Unit makeover

Correct each absurd statement:

  1. A mug holds 300 L.
  2. A car has a mass of 1,600 g.
  3. A table is 1.8 km long.
  4. A medicine dose is 5 L.
🛠️
Fix the unit
SOAKER Find an object that could sensibly be measured in either mL or L.
REVEALED ANSWER
300 mL; 1,600 kg; 1.8 m; 5 mL.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Which unit would you use for water collected from a dripping tap in 3 minutes?
2
Why?
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
Millilitres, because the quantity is likely much less than one litre and needs a precise small unit.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Tonight’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Due next lesson · answers unlocked in W5L4 · slide 2

2Unit for pencil length?
3Unit for road trip?
4Unit for a raindrop?
5Unit for a dog’s mass?
6Unit for a bottle of water?
7Pool volume — mL or L?
8Pencil — km or cm?
9Sensible unit for a tap drip session?
10Attribute first means…
11Unit for classroom length?
12Unit for road trip?
13Unit for a raindrop?
14Pool volume — mL or L?
15Unit for a bottle of water?
16Pencil — km or cm?
17Unit for a dog’s mass?
18Unit for pencil length?
19Sensible unit for a tap drip session?
20Attribute first means…
WEEK 5 · 26.1 · HOOK
TALK

Measuring litres and millilitres

Mystery container

A jug is half full. Is that 250 mL, 750 mL or 5 L?

You need the scale and the total capacity — not a guess from the picture alone.
HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W5L3
TEAMS

Mark last night’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
1Unit for classroom length?metres
2Unit for pencil length?centimetres
3Unit for road trip?kilometres
4Unit for a raindrop?millilitres
5Unit for a dog’s mass?kilograms
6Unit for a bottle of water?mL or L
7Pool volume — mL or L?L (or kL)
8Pencil — km or cm?cm
9Sensible unit for a tap drip session?mL
10Attribute first means…What are we measuring?
11Unit for classroom length?metres
12Unit for road trip?kilometres
13Unit for a raindrop?millilitres
14Pool volume — mL or L?L (or kL)
15Unit for a bottle of water?mL or L
16Pencil — km or cm?cm
17Unit for a dog’s mass?kilograms
18Unit for pencil length?centimetres
19Sensible unit for a tap drip session?mL
20Attribute first means…What are we measuring?
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
How many millilitres in 1 litre?
2
Which is greater: 850 mL or 1 L?
3
What does halfway between 600 and 800 mL represent?
Tomorrow: division — split into divisible parts.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🧪

We are learning to…

estimate, measure and convert capacities in litres and millilitres

Words we will use

litremillilitrescaleinterval

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Read scales by intervals

1,000 mL
500 mL
0 mL
Find labelled marks
Count equal intervals
Work out each interval
Read the liquid level
REVEALED ANSWER
The example jug shows about 680 mL if each small interval is 20 mL.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: Guessing the jug volume from “how full it looks”.
Read the scaleFind the intervalCapacity matters
REVEALED ANSWER
You need the scale marks and total capacity — a half-full jug is not always 500 mL.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

A 1 L jug has 10 equal intervals. The water reaches the seventh mark. What volume is shown?
1 L = 1,000 mLDivide by 10Multiply by 7
REVEALED ANSWER
Each interval is 100 mL. Seven intervals show 700 mL.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

1.5 L = ? mL
2,750 mL = ? L
0.4 L = ? mL
3 L 250 mL = ? mL
Use 1 L = 1,000 mL.
REVEALED ANSWER
1,500 mL; 2.75 L; 400 mL; 3,250 mL.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportOne jug diagram with labelled intervals only.
On-trackScale-reading workbook as set.
StretchA container holds 3 L. It is 65% full. Estimate the amount of water.

Estimate then measure

  1. Choose a cup, bottle or jug nearby.
  2. Estimate its capacity.
  3. Check the label or measure if safe.
  4. Record the difference.
💧
SA6 link
You will measure dripping water in mL.
SOAKER A container holds 3 L. It is 65% full. Estimate the amount of water.
REVEALED ANSWER
Answers vary. A useful response includes an estimate, measured value, correct unit and difference.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Convert 2.4 L to millilitres.
2
A tap loses 135 mL. Is that more or less than 0.2 L?
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
2,400 mL; 135 mL is less than 200 mL, so it is less than 0.2 L.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
600 ÷ 6
2
300 ÷ 6
3
36 ÷ 6
Tomorrow: division with remainders — context decides what to do with leftovers.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

📦

We are learning to…

divide large numbers by a one-digit divisor using efficient strategies

Words we will use

dividenddivisorquotientinverse

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Split into divisible parts

936 ÷ 6
= (600 ÷ 6) + (300 ÷ 6) + (36 ÷ 6)
= 100 + 50 + 6
= 156
Choose parts that are easy to divide by the divisor.
REVEALED ANSWER
936 ÷ 6 = 156.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: Splitting into parts that are still hard to divide.
Friendly partsDivide eachAdd quotients
REVEALED ANSWER
Choose parts divisible by the divisor (e.g. 900 + 36 for ÷6), then recombine.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Calculate 864 ÷ 4 using a split strategy and check by multiplication.
Split into friendly partsAdd quotientsMultiply answer by 4
REVEALED ANSWER
864 ÷ 4 = 200 + 16 = 216. Check: 216 × 4 = 864.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

735 ÷ 5
924 ÷ 7
1,248 ÷ 6
2,436 ÷ 4
Check each quotient with multiplication.
REVEALED ANSWER
147; 132; 208; 609.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportGiven split for 936 ÷ 6; students finish.
On-trackWorkbook division as set.
StretchCreate a division calculation with quotient 115.

Efficient strategy test

  1. 1,656 ÷ 8
  2. 1,472 ÷ 8
  3. 720 ÷ 6
  4. Choose split or written division and explain why.
➗
Check backwards
quotient × divisor = dividend
SOAKER Create a division calculation with quotient 115.
REVEALED ANSWER
207; 184; 120.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Calculate 1,656 ÷ 69.
2
Which multiplication fact checks your answer?
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
24; 69 × 24 = 1,656.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Tonight’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

9816 ÷ 4

11Split idea for ÷6 uses parts that are…

13484 ÷ 4
14725 ÷ 5
15846 ÷ 6

19963 ÷ 3
20Check: 156 × 6 = ?

TALK

Division with remainders

Bus-seat problem

257 students travel in buses that seat 8.

Is the answer 32 remainder 1 — or 33 buses?

TEAMS

Mark last night’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
1936 ÷ 6156
2484 ÷ 4121
3725 ÷ 5145
4846 ÷ 6141
5963 ÷ 3321
61656 ÷ 8207
7640 ÷ 5128
8504 ÷ 772
9816 ÷ 4204
10Check: 156 × 6 = ?936
11Split idea for ÷6 uses parts that are…Friendly multiples of 6
12936 ÷ 6156
13484 ÷ 4121
14725 ÷ 5145
15846 ÷ 6141
16640 ÷ 5128
17504 ÷ 772
18816 ÷ 4204
19963 ÷ 3321

WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
8 × 30
2
257 − 240
3
What does a remainder represent?
Tomorrow: SA6 mult/div revision — match strategy to the numbers.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

🚌

We are learning to…

calculate remainders and interpret them in real situations

Words we will use

remainderinterpretround upinverse check

Listen for these words in explanations.

EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

The context decides what to do

257 ÷ 8 = 32 r1
Objects left over → report r1People needing transport → round up to 33 buses
A remainder is not automatically rounded. Read the question.
REVEALED ANSWER
257 students need 33 buses.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: Always rounding the remainder up “to be safe”.
Read the storyLeftovers mean what?Inverse check
REVEALED ANSWER
Context decides: sometimes round up (buses), sometimes leftover is the answer, sometimes exact only.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Calculate 746 ÷ 6. Then explain the remainder in two different contexts.
Find quotient and remainderCheck: divisor × quotient + remainderRead the context
REVEALED ANSWER
746 ÷ 6 = 124 r2. It could mean 124 full packs and 2 left over, or 125 containers if every item must fit.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

389 ÷ 5
1,005 ÷ 8
672 ÷ 7
943 ÷ 9
Write quotient, remainder and check.
REVEALED ANSWER
77 r4; 125 r5; 96 exactly; 104 r7.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportTwo stories for the same division — different remainder actions.
On-trackRemainder problems as set.
StretchWrite two stories for 100 ÷ 6 that require different treatments of the remainder.
451 cupcakes packed 12 per tray
451 km travelled over 12 days
451 students in groups of 12
451 stickers shared among 12 students
For each, decide: keep remainder, use a decimal, or round up.
SOAKER Write two stories for 100 ÷ 6 that require different treatments of the remainder.
REVEALED ANSWER
Tray/groups: round up if all items/people need a place; daily distance could be 37.58… km; stickers may be 37 each with 7 left over.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Calculate 648 ÷ 6.
2
Does it have a remainder? Show the inverse check.
Write your answer in your notepad. Hold it up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
108 exactly; 108 × 6 = 648.
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Tonight’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Due next lesson · answers unlocked in W6L3 · slide 2
1257 ÷ 8 (buses seat 8) → buses needed?
2100 ÷ 6 exact?
3648 ÷ 6
4100 ÷ 6 leftover-apples story → leftover?
5Does remainder always round up?
630 ÷ 4
750 ÷ 7
890 ÷ 8
9Inverse check for 108 × 6
10257 ÷ 8 quotient only?
11648 ÷ 6
12100 ÷ 6 exact?
1330 ÷ 4
1450 ÷ 7
1590 ÷ 8
16Does remainder always round up?
17257 ÷ 8 (buses seat 8) → buses needed?
18Inverse check for 108 × 6
19100 ÷ 6 leftover-apples story → leftover?
20257 ÷ 8 quotient only?
WEEK 6 · Revision 1 · HOOK
TALK

SA6 revision: multiplication and division

Operation circuit

Four machines: ×1 digit, ×2 digits, ÷1 digit and unknown equations.

Your goal is not merely an answer — it is a clear, efficient strategy.
HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W6L2
TEAMS

Mark last night’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
1257 ÷ 8 (buses seat 8) → buses needed?33 buses (round up)
2100 ÷ 6 exact?16 r4
3648 ÷ 6108
4100 ÷ 6 leftover-apples story → leftover?4
5Does remainder always round up?No — depends on context
630 ÷ 47 r2
750 ÷ 77 r1
890 ÷ 811 r2
9Inverse check for 108 × 6648
10257 ÷ 8 quotient only?32 r1
11648 ÷ 6108
12100 ÷ 6 exact?16 r4
1330 ÷ 47 r2
1450 ÷ 77 r1
1590 ÷ 811 r2
16Does remainder always round up?No — depends on context
17257 ÷ 8 (buses seat 8) → buses needed?33 buses (round up)
18Inverse check for 108 × 6648
19100 ÷ 6 leftover-apples story → leftover?4
20257 ÷ 8 quotient only?32 r1
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
37 × 6
2
240 × 3
3
648 ÷ 6
Tomorrow: measurement, data and graphs — plan the investigation chain.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

💧

We are learning to…

use efficient multiplication and division (including unknowns) in drip-rate stories.

rateefficientunknowninverse

I’ll know I’ve got it when I can…

  • × and ÷ large numbers with clear working
  • solve missing-number equations
  • handle multi-step “twice as fast” problems
  • check with the inverse
EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Match the strategy to the numbers

Split
friendly place-value parts
Area model
organise two factors
Vertical algorithm
compact accurate method
Inverse
check or find unknown
Efficient does not mean shortest on paper. It means accurate, clear and sensible for the numbers.
REVEALED ANSWER
Different correct strategies may be equally efficient.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: “I multiplied the hours, but forgot the drip rate had already changed (e.g. twice as fast).”
Underline the rateAdjust rate firstThen × hours
REVEALED ANSWER
Change the rate before scaling time. Annotate a two-step chain.
SA6 PREP · × FLUENCY
NOTEBOOK

Drips in a short burst

A tap drips 42 times each minute. How many drips in 5 minutes? Show an efficient strategy.
REVEALED ANSWER
42 × 5 = 210 drips.
SA6 PREP · × FLUENCY
NOTEBOOK

Scale up by hours

The tap drips 180 times in one hour. How many times in 4 hours?
REVEALED ANSWER
180 × 4 = 720 drips.
SA6 PREP · ÷ FLUENCY
NOTEBOOK

Find the per-minute rate

There were 720 drips over 8 minutes. How many drips each minute?
REVEALED ANSWER
720 ÷ 8 = 90 drips per minute.
SA6 PREP · × 2-DIGIT
NOTEBOOK

A full day of drips

One tap drips 115 times every hour. How many drips in 24 hours?
REVEALED ANSWER
115 × 24 = 2760. (e.g. 115×20 + 115×4)
SA6 PREP · ÷ FLUENCY
NOTEBOOK

How long was it dripping?

There were 1656 drips in total. If each minute had 69 drips, how many minutes was the tap watched?
REVEALED ANSWER
1656 ÷ 69 = 24 minutes.
SA6 PREP · UNKNOWN
NOTEBOOK

Find the missing factor

64 × ___ = 1472
REVEALED ANSWER
1472 ÷ 64 = 23, so 64 × 23 = 1472.
SA6 PREP · MULTI-STEP
NOTEBOOK

Twice as fast

Your tap drips 95 times every hour. Another tap drips twice as fast. How many drops would the faster tap produce in 18 hours?
REVEALED ANSWER
Faster rate 190/hour. 190 × 18 = 3420 drips.
SA6 PREP · UNKNOWN
NOTEBOOK

Work the brackets first

(124 × 6) ÷ ___ = 93
REVEALED ANSWER
124 × 6 = 744. 744 ÷ ___ = 93 → ___ = 8.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

Solve (124 × 6) ÷ □ = 93. Show how you find the unknown.
Calculate brackets first744 ÷ ? = 93Use inverse multiplication
REVEALED ANSWER
744 ÷ 93 = 8, so the unknown is 8.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

4,205 × 6
243 × 16
142 × 18
1,656 ÷ 69
Estimate, calculate, then inverse-check.
REVEALED ANSWER
25,230; 3,888; 2,556; 24.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportCompare two methods for one station; tick clearer.
On-trackCircuit stations as planned.
StretchFind a second method for one station and compare efficiency.
A
42 drops/min for 5 min
B
115 drips/hour for 24 h
C
1,656 ÷ 69
D
(124 × 6) ÷ □ = 93
SOAKER Find a second method for one station and compare efficiency.
REVEALED ANSWER
210; 2,760; 24; 8.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
115 drips/hour. How many in 24 hours?
2
64 × ___ = 1472
Tomorrow: measurement, data and graphs for the investigation.
Show working. Hold up when asked.
REVEALED ANSWER
1) 2760 2) 23
HOMEWORK · 20 PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Tonight’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Due W6L4 · answers on slide 2
142 drips each minute. Drips in 5 minutes?
2A tap drips 180 times in an hour. Times in 4 hours?

51656 drips. If 69 per minute, how many minutes?
6Solve: 64 × ___ = 1472

8Solve: (124 × 6) ÷ ___ = 93
936 × 40
10214 × 15
11936 ÷ 6
12484 ÷ 4
1342 drips/min × 5 min
14180/hour × 4 hours
15720 ÷ 8
16115 × 24
171656 ÷ 69
1864 × ___ = 1472
1995×2×18
20(124 × 6) ÷ ___ = 93
WEEK 6 · Revision 2 · HOOK
TALK

SA6 revision: measurement, data and graphs

One dripping tap

A tiny drip looks harmless. Over 24 hours, it may waste litres.

How can a three-minute sample help us predict a whole day?
HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W6L3
TEAMS

Mark last night’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
142 drips each minute. Drips in 5 minutes?210
2A tap drips 180 times in an hour. Times in 4 hours?720
3720 drips over 8 minutes. Drips each minute?90
4One tap: 115 drips/hour. Drips in 24 hours?2760
51656 drips. If 69 per minute, how many minutes?24
6Solve: 64 × ___ = 147223
7A faster tap drips twice as fast as 95/hour. Faster tap in 18 hours?3420
8Solve: (124 × 6) ÷ ___ = 938
936 × 401440
10214 × 153210
11936 ÷ 6156
12484 ÷ 4121
1342 drips/min × 5 min210
14180/hour × 4 hours720
15720 ÷ 890
16115 × 242760
171656 ÷ 6924
1864 × ___ = 147223
1995×2×183420
20(124 × 6) ÷ ___ = 938
WAKE UP YOUR MATHS BRAIN
TEAMS

Retrieval sprint

1
Which unit suits a small amount of collected water?
2
How many 3-minute intervals are in 60 minutes?
3
What makes a graph easy to read?
Next: SA6 investigation sessions — collect, graph, calculate, submit.
Answer aloud first. One student may type the class answer in Teams.
LEARNING INTENTION
WATCH

Today’s target

📊

We are learning to…

plan the drip investigation: units, the ×480 scale, tables and two graph types.

sample×480tablebarline

I’ll know I’ve got it when I can…

  • explain why we multiply by 480
  • scale a 3-minute sample to 24 hours
  • choose sensible units (mL / L)
  • match graph type to the question
EXPLICIT TEACHING
WATCH

Plan the investigation chain

Collect for 3 min
→
Record mL in a table
→
Scale to 24 h
→
Create 2 graph types
→
Interpret a typical result
24 hours contains 480 groups of 3 minutes because 24 × 60 ÷ 3 = 480.
REVEALED ANSWER
Multiply a 3-minute amount by 480 to estimate 24-hour wastage.
WATCH OUT
WATCH

Common trap

Trap: “I multiplied my 3-minute amount by 24 because there are 24 hours.”
3 min → hour?Hours → day20 × 24 = 480
REVEALED ANSWER
Multiply by 480 (groups of 3 minutes in a day), not by 24.
SA6 PREP · UNITS
NOTEBOOK

Sensible units

Sample jar shows ~55 mL — unit?
24-hour total ~12 L — unit?
Write 2500 mL in litres
Write 0.8 L in millilitres
REVEALED ANSWER
mL for small samples; L for day totals. 2500 mL = 2.5 L; 0.8 L = 800 mL.
SA6 PREP · SCALE
WATCH

The 3-minute → 24-hour bridge

Why do we multiply a 3-minute amount by 480 to estimate 24 hours?
Step 11 hour = 60 min
60 ÷ 3 = 20
Step 224 hours
24 × 20 = 480
Step 33-min amount × 480
= day estimate
REVEALED ANSWER
24 × 60 ÷ 3 = 480 groups of 3 minutes in a day.
SA6 PREP · SCALE
NOTEBOOK

Scale a practice sample

Practice sample (not your SA6 tap): 22 mL collected in 3 minutes.
Estimate wastage in 24 hours. Give the answer in mL and in L.
REVEALED ANSWER
22 × 480 = 10 560 mL = 10.56 L.
SA6 PREP · DATA
NOTEBOOK

Build a comparison table

Practice class data (different from SA6). Add a classmate row and a “you” row in your book.
PersonWater in 3 minutes
Ari38 mL
Ben72 mL
Cara110 mL
Drew155 mL
Eve41 mL
Finn50 mL
Classmate
You (practice)
REVEALED ANSWER
Tables need clear labels and consistent units (mL).
SA6 PREP · GRAPHS
TALK

Two graphs, two jobs

Graph A — compare people

Which type best compares each person’s 3-minute total?

Graph B — change over time

Which type best shows wastage building across 24 hours?

REVEALED ANSWER
Compare people → bar/column. Change over a day → line graph. Titles, axes, units matter.
SA6 PREP · GRAPHS
NOTEBOOK

Sketch one graph

Using the practice table, sketch a bar graph of the six named results. Label axes and units.
REVEALED ANSWER
Bars for Ari→Finn; vertical axis in mL; title e.g. “Water collected in 3 minutes”.
SA6 PREP · READY
WATCH

Investigation checklist

□ Measure 3 minutes carefully
□ Record in mL
□ Scale with ×480
□ Convert to L if sensible
□ Table with labels
□ Two different graphs
□ Show efficient working
□ Check unknowns with inverse
Next: SA6 Session 1 — collect your own drip data.
REVEALED ANSWER
Students self-check the list before Session 1.
LIVE ANNOTATION
NOTEBOOK

Work it out with me

A tap loses 45 mL in 3 minutes. Estimate the loss in 24 hours and convert to litres.
Find 480 intervals45 × 480Convert mL to L
REVEALED ANSWER
45 × 480 = 21,600 mL = 21.6 L.
WE DO
TALK

Guided challenge

Column graph
Compare each person’s result
Dot plot
Show clustering and repeated values
Table
Keep exact values organised
Typical value
Choose and justify a representative result
Every graph needs a title, labelled axes, even scale and accurate plotting.
REVEALED ANSWER
A column graph and dot plot are suitable different representations for the discrete numerical results.
INDEPENDENT PRACTICE
NOTEBOOK

Your turn

SupportFill the conversions: min → hour → day.
On-trackInvestigation-planning tasks as set.
StretchExplain why a graph scale that jumps by 100 mL may hide differences near 45 mL.

Dummy data practice

Water collected in 3 min: 45, 80, 135, 170, 45, 47 mL.

  1. Draw a quick dot plot.
  2. Identify a typical value and justify it.
  3. Predict 24-hour loss for 80 mL.
📈
Use a sensible scale
SOAKER Explain why a graph scale that jumps by 100 mL may hide differences near 45 mL.
REVEALED ANSWER
45 is the mode; the median is 63.5 mL. A student may justify either as “typical” if reasoning is clear. 80 × 480 = 38,400 mL = 38.4 L.
CHECK FOR UNDERSTANDING
NOTEBOOK

Exit ticket

1
Why multiply a 3-minute sample by 480?
2
22 mL in 3 min → mL in 24 h?
Tomorrow: SA6 Session 1 — collect your own drip data.
Explain in a sentence + calculation.
REVEALED ANSWER
1) 480 groups of 3 min in 24 h. 2) 22 × 480 = 10 560 mL.

NOTEBOOK

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

719200 mL = ? L

133-min intervals in 24 hours?

1540 mL × 480
1610560 mL = ? L

18Bar or line for change over 24 hours?

2024 × 60 ÷ 3 = ?

ASSESS

SA6 · Session 1: collect data

SA6

Down the Drain

Plan and conduct a statistical investigation. Work individually, but your class will combine results in the table.

HOMEWORK ANSWERS · W6L4
TEAMS

Mark last night’s practice

There's only one way to improve anything — practice!

Tick / fix in books — celebrate improvement through practice.
13-min intervals in 1 hour?20
23-min intervals in 24 hours?480
324 × 60 ÷ 3 = ?480
4If 22 mL in 3 min, ≈ mL in 24 h?10560 mL
510560 mL = ? L10.56 L

719200 mL = ? L19.2 L

9Better unit for 24-hour wastage ~15 L: mL or L?L
10Name a graph for comparing people's 3-min amountsBar / column
11Name a graph for wastage building over a dayLine graph
12Why might two taps give different 3-min totals?Drip rate differs
133-min intervals in 24 hours?480
1422 mL × 48010560 mL
1540 mL × 48019200 mL
1610560 mL = ? L10.56 L

18Bar or line for change over 24 hours?Line

2024 × 60 ÷ 3 = ?480
ASSESSMENT CRITERIA
WATCH

What success looks like

Plan and conduct
Collect discrete numerical data carefully.
Measure
Use a suitable unit and accurate reading.
Calculate
Use an efficient multiplication strategy to scale from 3 minutes to 24 hours.
SUPERVISED WORK
ASSESS

Assessment page 1

Work directly in your assessment document

Use this screen only to enlarge the page or clarify where you are up to.

□ Use a safe container and stable setup.
□ Collect for exactly 3 minutes.
□ Record the amount in mL.
□ Add one classmate result and your own result.
□ Show the calculation used to scale to 24 hours.
SUPERVISED WORK
ASSESS

Assessment page 2

Work directly in your assessment document

Use this screen only to enlarge the page or clarify where you are up to.

□ Use a safe container and stable setup.
□ Collect for exactly 3 minutes.
□ Record the amount in mL.
□ Add one classmate result and your own result.
□ Show the calculation used to scale to 24 hours.

Before you submit

Use a safe container and stable setup.
Collect for exactly 3 minutes.
Record the amount in mL.
Add one classmate result and your own result.
Show the calculation used to scale to 24 hours.

WEEK 7 · SA6 · Session 2 · ASSESSMENT
ASSESS

SA6 · Session 2: graph the data

SA6

Two different representations

Use graph paper for one graph and a digital graph maker for the other. Add the digital graph to your document.

ASSESSMENT CRITERIA
WATCH

What success looks like

Represent
Use digital tools to represent discrete numerical data.
Accuracy
Use labels, scale and plotted values correctly.
Interpret
Explain what a typical quantity might be and why.
SUPERVISED WORK
ASSESS

Assessment page 3

Work directly in your assessment document

Use this screen only to enlarge the page or clarify where you are up to.

□ Use two genuinely different graph types.
□ Add a clear title.
□ Label both axes and units.
□ Choose an even, sensible scale.
□ Plot every class result accurately.
□ Insert or snip the digital graph into the document.
FINAL CHECK
ASSESS

Before you submit

Use two genuinely different graph types.
Add a clear title.
Label both axes and units.
Choose an even, sensible scale.
Plot every class result accurately.
Insert or snip the digital graph into the document.
My name is on the document.
I have answered every page assigned today.
Do not reveal or discuss answers during supervised assessment.
WEEK 7 · SA6 · Session 3 · ASSESSMENT
ASSESS

SA6 · Session 3: Part B calculations

SA6

Show efficient strategies

Read each problem carefully. Write equations, calculations and units where relevant.

ASSESSMENT CRITERIA
WATCH

What success looks like

Fluency
Multiply large numbers by one- and two-digit numbers.
Division
Divide large numbers by single-digit numbers.
Unknowns
Apply number properties to solve numerical equations.
SUPERVISED WORK
ASSESS

Assessment page 4

Work directly in your assessment document

Use this screen only to enlarge the page or clarify where you are up to.

□ Show all calculations.
□ Use multiplication or division that matches the situation.
□ Check with an inverse operation.
□ Solve unknowns, not only the bracketed calculation.
□ Label contextual answers.
FINAL CHECK
ASSESS

Before you submit

Show all calculations.
Use multiplication or division that matches the situation.
Check with an inverse operation.
Solve unknowns, not only the bracketed calculation.
Label contextual answers.

ASSESS

SA6 · Session 4: finish and submit

SA6

Final calculations and quality check

Complete the final page, then revisit every table, graph and calculation before submitting.

ASSESSMENT CRITERIA
WATCH

What success looks like

Purposeful problem-solving
Use a planned approach and make progress towards solutions.
Efficient strategies
Select methods that suit the numbers.
Complete investigation
Connect measurement, data, graphs and calculations.
SUPERVISED WORK
ASSESS

Use this screen only to enlarge the page or clarify where you are up to.

□ Every question is answered.
□ All calculations are visible.
□ Graphs have titles, labels and sensible scales.
□ The 24-hour answer has a unit.
□ The investigation explanation is clear.
□ The document contains both graphs.
